opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was doing squats, and suddenly this sharp, stabbing pain hit my left side so hard it dropped me to the floor, and now I can't stop throwing up.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Sudden severe left-sided pelvic pain during exercise with recurrent vomiting.

symptom_narrative.onset: sudden, 2 hours ago during a workout
symptom_narrative.duration: 2 hours, rapidly worsening from intermittent to constant
symptom_narrative.character: sharp, stabbing, severe pain, initially colicky then constant
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: agonizing, dropped me to the floor
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: sudden onset, worsening, exacerbated by position changes
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: lying still in the fetal position
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: severe nausea, recurrent vomiting since the pain started
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: no previous episodes like this, no known ovarian cysts
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: lying on the couch curled up, groaning in severe pain

current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: took one dose with no effect

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: roommate present, regular menstrual cycle, no known pregnancy risk factors reported

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened, wants pain relief and to know what's happening

access_barriers.transport: roommate available to assist but patient in too much pain to self-transport comfortably
